dominant_palette: black, white, gold, grey, cobalt blue
composition_vocabulary: Tiles lean heavily toward centred subjects on clean or minimal grounds — product shots, isolated objects, single symbols. Occasional editorial crowding (sketches, stacked packaging) but the dominant mode is object-on-ground with generous negative space.
lighting_quality: Predominantly flat studio or neutral ambient light; a few tiles use soft directional light on metallic or three-dimensional objects, but chiaroscuro is absent
material_textures: polished gold metal, matte card/paperboard, glossy plastic, brushed or raw leather, smooth ceramic, printed paper
aesthetic_register: The sample sits at the intersection of graphic-design / art-direction school and luxury brand identity — closer to a designer's reference archive for packaging and logomark systems than a traditional jewellery moodboard
notably_absent: Intimate skin contact with jewellery — no wrist, neck, ear, or finger shots wearing a piece — and no soft organic materials like velvet, draped fabric, or florals typically anchoring luxury jewellery mood references
